qa_pairs.question: What types of resources are accessible to researchers through the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: Through the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC), researchers can request access to general-purpose systems, large shared memory systems, general-purpose computing on graphics processing units, storage, and cloud resources.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of technical support is provided to researchers who receive an allocation through the competition?
qa_pairs.answer: Technical support is available to assist researchers with installing and configuring software packages, code optimization, and detailed advice on solving technical issues. Federation staff may write some code, but deep “embedded” programming support is not included; assistance is generally limited to problems requiring a few days of effort.

qa_pairs.question: Is it necessary for applicants to consult with technical staff before submitting their Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is strongly recommended that all applicants consult with technical staff responsible for the clusters they intend to use. This consultation ensures that the technical aspects of the proposals match well with the requested clusters, benefiting the Principal Investigator (PI). PIs who have not previously used the clusters should especially discuss their proposal with technical staff before submission, allowing time for discussion or revisions.
